projectDescription: A university-level Computer Vision course (taught at PUC by Domingo Mery) covering computational geometry, deep learning architectures, and AI ethics with MATLAB and Python examples.
keyFeatures: Computational geometry with homogeneous coordinates, 2D/3D transformations, and homographies
Camera calibration and parameter estimation (RANSAC, least squares, SVD)
Epipolar and trifocal geometry for multi-view 3D reconstruction
Deep learning with CNNs, YOLO object detection, and facial recognition
Generative models including GANs and Stable Diffusion
Vision Transformers (ViT) and CLIP for vision-language tasks
AI ethics: fairness, bias, explainability, and adversarial attacks
Hands-on Python (Google Colab) and MATLAB examples for each topic
Recorded lecture videos from prior course editions (2021)
publicApiSurface: Cap01: Introduction — definitions and history of computer vision
Cap02: Geometry — homogeneous coordinates, transformations 2D/3D, calibration, homographies, epipolar geometry, trifocal geometry
Cap03: Deep Learning — CNNs, YOLO, facial analysis, UNet segmentation, GANs, Transformers, Stable Diffusion, CLIP, anomaly detection
Cap04: Ethics — fairness, bias, explainability (MinPlus), federated/swarm learning, good practices
MATLAB examples: CV02_3DPyramid, CV02_Calibration, CV02_EpiDemo_3D, CV02_TrifocalGeometry, CV02_SIFT, CV02_HomografiaRANSAC, CV02_Trans2D
Python/Colab notebooks: CNN training, YOLO detection, facial analysis with AdaFace, UNet segmentation, GAN image generation, Transformers with HuggingFace
